Title: Mark a living patient as deceased with a coded cause of death and a valid death date
Preconditions: Logged-in clinician with edit-patient privilege; an active living patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient record | 2. Open the Mark Patient Deceased action | 3. Toggle Dead to Yes | 4. Enter Death Date within the allowed range | 5. Select a coded Cause of Death from the concept dictionary | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Patient is flagged deceased; the deceased banner/indicator appears; dead=true, deathDate and causeOfDeath persisted with the selected concept

Title: Mark deceased using free-text Cause of Death when Other/Unknown concept is selected
Preconditions: Clinician on a living patient; the dictionary contains an Other non-coded cause concept
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Set Dead=Yes | 3. Enter valid Death Date | 4. Select Cause=Other (non-coded) | 5. Enter free-text cause | 6. Save
Test_Data: Death Date=2026-05-01; Cause=Other; CauseText=Witnessed cardiac arrest at home
Expected_Result: Patient marked deceased; causeOfDeath stored as the Other concept and the free-text non-coded value is captured and displayed

Title: Reject marking deceased without selecting a cause of death when cause is mandatory
Preconditions: Clinician; the death form configured to require cause of death
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Set Dead=Yes | 3. Enter valid Death Date | 4. Leave Cause of Death empty | 5. Attempt Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation error indicating cause of death is required; patient remains living

Title: Reject a future death date (after current system date)
Preconditions: Clinician on a living patient; system date is known
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Set Dead=Yes | 3. Enter a Death Date in the future | 4. Select a valid cause | 5. Attempt Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a validation error that death date cannot be in the future; patient remains living

Title: Reject a death date earlier than the patient birth date
Preconditions: Clinician; patient has a recorded birth date
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Set Dead=Yes | 3. Enter a Death Date before the birth date | 4. Select valid cause | 5. Attempt Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected; validation indicates death date cannot precede birth date; patient remains living

Title: Accept a death date equal to the birth date (same-day neonatal death)
Preconditions: Clinician; neonatal patient with birth date today recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Set Dead=Yes | 3. Enter Death Date equal to birth date | 4. Select a perinatal cause | 5. Save
Test_Data: Birth Date=2026-07-01; Death Date=2026-07-01; Cause=Concept:Perinatal condition
Expected_Result: Save succeeds; patient marked deceased; zero-day lifespan handled without error

Title: Accept a death date equal to the current system date (boundary today)
Preconditions: Clinician on a living patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Set Dead=Yes | 3. Enter Death Date = today | 4. Select valid cause | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Save succeeds; death date today is accepted as the upper boundary

Title: Reject a malformed or non-existent calendar death date
Preconditions: Clinician on a living patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Set Dead=Yes | 3. Enter an invalid calendar date | 4. Attempt Save
Expected_Result: Date is rejected by input validation; no save occurs; clear error shown

Title: Validate Dead flag, date and cause combinations via decision table
Preconditions: Clinician; death form available
Test_Steps: 1. For each rule set Dead/Date/Cause as specified | 2. Attempt Save | 3. Record acceptance or rejection
Test_Data: R1 Dead=Yes,Date=valid,Cause=coded=>Accept; R2 Dead=Yes,Date=empty,Cause=coded=>Reject; R3 Dead=Yes,Date=valid,Cause=empty=>Reject(if required); R4 Dead=No,Date=set,Cause=set=>Date/Cause cleared/ignored; R5 Dead=Yes,Date=future=>Reject
Expected_Result: System enforces each decision rule exactly; only valid combinations persist and invalid ones are rejected with appropriate messages

Title: Reverse an erroneously recorded death by setting Dead back to No
Preconditions: Clinician with edit privilege; a patient currently flagged deceased
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient | 2. Open Mark Patient Deceased action | 3. Toggle Dead to No | 4. Save | 5. Reopen the record
Expected_Result: Patient transitions deceased->living; deceased banner removed; deathDate and causeOfDeath cleared or nulled per config; change is audited

Title: Verify death-derived data is reconciled after reversing deceased status
Preconditions: A patient was reversed from deceased to living (see reverse flow)
Test_Steps: 1. Reverse the deceased flag to No | 2. Inspect deathDate, causeOfDeath and any deceased indicators | 3. Inspect previously auto-cancelled future appointments
Test_Data: Patient previously dead with cancelled future appointments
Expected_Result: Death attributes are cleared; UI no longer shows deceased; previously cancelled appointments are NOT silently auto-restored and require manual review

Title: Confirm both mark-deceased and reverse actions are captured in the audit trail
Preconditions: Auditing/logging enabled; admin with audit access
Test_Steps: 1. Mark a patient deceased | 2. Reverse the deceased flag | 3. Open the audit/log view or query the change log
Test_Data: Patient marked then reversed by user clinician1
Expected_Result: Audit log records both transitions with actor, timestamp, old and new values for dead, deathDate and causeOfDeath

Title: Future scheduled appointments are cancelled or flagged when a patient is marked deceased
Preconditions: Patient has one or more future appointments scheduled; appointment module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule a future appointment for a living patient | 2. Mark the patient deceased | 3. Open the appointment list
Expected_Result: Future appointments are cancelled or clearly flagged as deceased; no active future appointment remains bookable for the patient

Title: Block new appointment scheduling for a deceased patient
Preconditions: Patient is flagged deceased; scheduler with booking privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient | 2. Attempt to schedule a new future appointment | 3. Attempt Save
Expected_Result: Scheduling is blocked or requires explicit override with warning that the patient is deceased; no silent booking

Title: Active drug and lab orders are discontinued or flagged on patient death
Preconditions: Patient has active drug orders and pending lab orders; orders module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Place active drug and lab orders for a living patient | 2. Mark the patient deceased | 3. Review the active orders list
Test_Data: Active drug order + pending lab order present; Death Date=2026-07-01
Expected_Result: Active and future-dated orders are discontinued or flagged for review; clinicians are alerted; no medication continues active against a deceased patient

Title: Block placing new active medication orders for a deceased patient
Preconditions: Patient flagged deceased; provider with ordering privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient | 2. Attempt to place a new active drug order | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Order placement is blocked or requires an explicit justified override; deceased status is surfaced to the ordering provider

Title: Open active visit handling when a patient is marked deceased
Preconditions: Patient has an open active visit; clinician
Test_Steps: 1. Ensure the patient has an active open visit | 2. Mark the patient deceased with death date within the visit window | 3. Review visit status
Expected_Result: Active visit is closed/stopped at or after the death datetime or flagged consistently; no active visit persists indefinitely for a deceased patient

Title: Void a patient record and verify it is excluded from searches and active lists
Preconditions: Admin/superuser with void privilege; a non-deceased test patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Invoke Delete/Void with a reason | 3. Confirm | 4. Search for the patient in normal find-patient
Expected_Result: Patient is voided (soft-deleted); excluded from standard search and active patient lists; record retained in database with voided=true, voidReason, voidedBy, dateVoided

Title: Reject voiding a patient without providing a void reason
Preconditions: Admin with void privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Invoke Void | 3. Leave the reason blank | 4. Attempt to confirm
Expected_Result: Void is blocked; validation requires a non-empty void reason; record remains active

Title: Unvoid a previously voided patient and restore visibility
Preconditions: A voided patient exists; admin with manage privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Locate the voided patient via admin/manage | 2. Invoke Unvoid/Restore | 3. Search in normal find-patient
Expected_Result: Patient is restored; voided=false; reappears in standard searches and active lists; restoration is audited

Title: Voiding a patient cascades to associated encounters, visits and observations
Preconditions: Patient with encounters, visits and obs; admin with DB/audit visibility
Test_Steps: 1. Note encounter/visit/obs IDs for the patient | 2. Void the patient | 3. Inspect associated encounter, visit and obs voided flags
Expected_Result: Associated encounters, visits and observations are marked voided with consistent voidReason and dateVoided; no orphaned active child records remain

Title: Retire a death-related metadata concept and verify it is hidden from new entry but preserved on existing records
Preconditions: Admin with manage-concepts privilege; an active cause-of-death concept used on a record
Test_Steps: 1. Mark a patient deceased using a cause concept | 2. Retire that concept with a reason | 3. Attempt to select it for a new death entry | 4. View the existing deceased patient
Test_Data: Concept=Old Cause Code; Retire Reason=Replaced by ICD mapping
Expected_Result: Retired concept is unavailable for new selections but historical records still display the original value; retiredBy/dateRetired/retireReason set

Title: Differentiate retire (metadata) from void (data) behavior via decision table
Preconditions: Admin with both privileges
Test_Steps: 1. For metadata object apply Retire and check new-entry availability and historical display | 2. For patient data apply Void and check search visibility | 3. Compare semantics
Expected_Result: Retire hides metadata from new use while preserving history; Void soft-deletes data from active views; neither performs a hard delete; behaviors are not interchangeable

Title: Archive a deceased patient record after the configured active-retention window
Preconditions: Patient deceased beyond the configured active window; archival policy configured
Test_Steps: 1. Identify a deceased patient past the active-retention threshold | 2. Run/trigger the archival job or workflow | 3. Verify record moved to archived state
Test_Data: Death Date older than active window threshold
Expected_Result: Record transitions to archived state; remains retrievable for legal/clinical retrieval but is excluded from day-to-day active workflows

Title: Enforce minimum legal retention period before any purge of a deceased record
Preconditions: Retention policy with a defined minimum retention period from death date
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to purge a deceased record before the retention minimum elapses | 2. Attempt to purge a record after the retention period elapses
Test_Data: Retention=10 years post-death; Record A death=2025-01-01; Record B death=2010-01-01
Expected_Result: Purge is blocked for Record A (within retention) and only permitted for Record B (past retention); boundary at exactly the retention date is enforced per policy

Title: Validate retention boundary on the exact expiry day (day-of vs day-after)
Preconditions: Retention policy defined to the day
Test_Steps: 1. Set record death date so retention expires exactly today | 2. Evaluate purge eligibility on expiry day | 3. Evaluate on day after
Expected_Result: Eligibility honors the documented inclusive/exclusive boundary consistently; no off-by-one purge of still-protected records

Title: Verify audit log immutability and completeness for the full death lifecycle
Preconditions: Auditing enabled; admin audit access
Test_Steps: 1. Perform mark-deceased, reverse, void, unvoid, retire on test objects | 2. Open audit trail | 3. Attempt to edit or delete an audit entry
Test_Data: Sequence of lifecycle actions by clinician1/admin1
Expected_Result: Every action is logged with actor, timestamp and before/after values; audit entries cannot be edited or deleted via the UI

Title: Enforce privilege checks for mark-deceased, void and retire actions
Preconditions: Users provisioned: clinician(no edit-patient), data-clerk, admin
Test_Steps: 1. As a user lacking the required privilege attempt mark-deceased | 2. Attempt void | 3. Attempt retire | 4. As privileged admin repeat
Expected_Result: Each restricted action is denied for under-privileged users (403/no UI affordance) and permitted only for authorized roles; denials are logged

Title: Prevent privilege escalation when reversing deceased or unvoiding via direct API
Preconditions: API access; a low-privilege token without edit-patient
Test_Steps: 1. Obtain a low-privilege session/token | 2. Call the REST endpoint to set dead=false or void=false directly | 3. Inspect response
Test_Data: REST PUT to patient with dead=false using clerk token
Expected_Result: API rejects the unauthorized modification with 403; no state change occurs; attempt is audited

Title: Set deceased status via OpenMRS REST and verify persisted attributes
Preconditions: REST enabled; privileged token; living test patient uuid
Test_Steps: 1. PUT/POST to /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid} or person with dead, deathDate, causeOfDeath | 2. GET the patient | 3. Inspect fields
Expected_Result: API persists dead, deathDate and causeOfDeath; subsequent GET returns the same values with correct types

Title: Reject deceased update with an invalid causeOfDeath concept uuid
Preconditions: REST enabled; privileged token
Test_Steps: 1. PUT patient with dead=true and a non-existent causeOfDeath uuid | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API returns a 4xx validation error; patient is not marked deceased with the invalid reference

Title: Expose deceased status via FHIR Patient.deceasedDateTime/deceasedBoolean
Preconditions: FHIR R4 module enabled; a deceased patient with a death date
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Patient/{id} | 2. Inspect the deceased[x] element
Expected_Result: FHIR Patient returns deceasedDateTime=2026-06-15 (or deceasedBoolean=true when no date); element conforms to FHIR R4 Patient deceased[x] cardinality

Title: Represent deceased without a known date as deceasedBoolean=true
Preconditions: FHIR R4 enabled; patient flagged dead with no death date
Test_Steps: 1. Mark patient dead=true without a death date (if permitted) | 2. GET the FHIR Patient | 3. Inspect deceased[x]
Expected_Result: FHIR returns deceasedBoolean=true and does not emit a fabricated deceasedDateTime

Title: Reverse deceased clears deceased[x] in the FHIR projection
Preconditions: FHIR R4 enabled; a deceased patient subsequently reversed to living
Test_Steps: 1. Reverse the patient deceased status to living | 2. GET the FHIR Patient | 3. Inspect deceased[x]
Expected_Result: FHIR Patient no longer contains deceasedDateTime/deceasedBoolean=true; resource reflects living status; projection is consistent

Title: Outbound HL7 ADT^A03 discharge/death message carries patient death disposition
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine configured; outbound ADT feed for the SUT
Test_Steps: 1. Mark a patient deceased | 2. Capture the outbound HL7 message | 3. Inspect PID-29 (death date), PID-30 (death indicator) and PV1-36 discharge disposition
Expected_Result: ADT message populates PID-29 death date, PID-30 death indicator=Y and an Expired discharge disposition consistent with HL7 v2 semantics

Title: Inbound HL7 death notification updates patient deceased status idempotently
Preconditions: HL7 inbound listener configured; mapping for death fields
Test_Steps: 1. Send an inbound ADT message with PID-29/PID-30 death fields | 2. Re-send the identical message | 3. Inspect patient state
Expected_Result: Patient is marked deceased on first message; re-processing the identical message is idempotent and does not duplicate or corrupt the death record

Title: Pairwise coverage of role, channel, date-validity and downstream-config
Preconditions: Multiple roles, channels (UI/REST/HL7), and config flags available
Test_Steps: 1. Execute combinations: (Admin,UI,validDate,cascade-on),(Clinician,REST,futureDate,cascade-off),(Clerk,HL7,validDate,cascade-on),(Admin,REST,invalidDate,cascade-off) | 2. Record outcome per pairing
Test_Data: Role x Channel x DateValidity x CascadeConfig pairs
Expected_Result: Each pairing behaves per its constraints: authorized+valid persists with correct cascade; unauthorized or invalid-date pairings are rejected; no pairing produces inconsistent state

Title: Mark-deceased dialog is keyboard navigable and screen-reader accessible
Preconditions: Screen reader and keyboard-only environment; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Mark Patient Deceased dialog using keyboard only | 2. Tab through Dead toggle, date and cause fields | 3. Trigger a validation error | 4. Verify SR announcements and focus order
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard; labels and required-field/error messages are announced; focus order is logical; meets WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Deceased indicator is clearly and consistently surfaced across patient views
Preconditions: A deceased patient; access to dashboard, search results and header banner
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient dashboard | 2. View the header/banner | 3. Locate the patient in search results | 4. Open a clinical form
Expected_Result: A consistent, prominent deceased indicator (banner/badge) is shown across dashboard, header, search results and clinical entry points to prevent ordering errors

Title: Exploratory probe of conflicting and concurrent death edits
Preconditions: Two clinician sessions on the same patient; test environment
Test_Steps: 1. Open the same living patient in two sessions | 2. In session A mark deceased and save | 3. In session B (stale view) attempt to edit demographics and save | 4. Observe conflict handling
Expected_Result: Concurrency is handled safely (optimistic-lock/version error or last-consistent-write); no lost-update silently overwrites the death record; user is informed of the conflict

Title: Verify person table death attributes and child voided flags after lifecycle actions
Preconditions: DB read access to test instance; lifecycle actions performed
Test_Steps: 1. Query person row for dead, death_date, cause_of_death, changed_by, date_changed after mark-deceased | 2. Void the patient | 3. Query voided, voided_by, date_voided, void_reason on person and child encounter/obs rows
Expected_Result: Death attributes are stored with correct audit columns; void sets voided=1 with reason/actor/timestamp and cascades to children; no hard deletes occur

Title: Open billing/charges are flagged and new charges blocked on patient death
Preconditions: Billing module enabled; patient with open charges; death applied
Test_Steps: 1. Ensure the patient has open/unsettled charges | 2. Mark the patient deceased | 3. Review billing status | 4. Attempt to add a new post-death dated charge
Expected_Result: Open charges are flagged for estate/settlement handling; adding new service charges dated after death is blocked or warned; financial integrity preserved

Title: Access to a deceased patient record still enforces PHI authorization and minimum-necessary
Preconditions: Deceased patient; users with and without legitimate access
Test_Steps: 1. As an authorized user open the deceased record | 2. As an unauthorized user attempt access | 3. Verify access is logged
Expected_Result: Deceased records remain PHI-protected; unauthorized access is denied; all access is audited per minimum-necessary; post-mortem privacy obligations are honored

Title: Decision table for reverse-deceased eligibility and downstream reconciliation
Preconditions: Deceased patients in varied downstream states
Test_Steps: 1. R1 deceased with no downstream effects -> reverse | 2. R2 deceased with cancelled appts -> reverse + manual review | 3. R3 deceased with discontinued orders -> reverse + no auto-reactivation | 4. R4 archived/purged -> reverse blocked
Expected_Result: Reverse succeeds where eligible and clears death attributes; downstream items are not auto-reactivated; reverse is blocked once record is archived/purged beyond reversible state
